54 Munster has come into its own again
For the past six years the} Southern pro-
Vinee has vanquished the Eastern in the
All-Ireland Hurling Final, Where Tip
perary, Limerick, and Cork have failed,

Despits the ‘bad times” Croke Park
fiiewed a tremendous hosting’ of Gaels
‘ou Suriday last, andthe greatest enthusiasm
prevailed when the ball was put in motion
for the Clare v. Leix game. As the minutes
passed this enthusiasm showed no signs of
‘abatement, because the spirit of partizan-
ship was abroad, and an Irish crowd is
never so vehement as when imbued with
partizan feelings. Personally, while the
truly National character of the gathering
evoked a responsive feeling of warmth,
must confess the game enthused me very

A. Drawback

Ta the first place, the number of times the
play had to be stopped to award frees to one
side or the other was altogether out of pro-
Portion to the fitness of things. tis di
cult to fix the responsibility for this. Ie
was certainly not the fault of the referee,
Mr. Jobn Lalor, Kilkenny, who interpreted
the fules to the satisfaction of everybody.
Neither was the game a rough one, in fact
it was not even vigorous except for a brief
space in the last half. The fouls committed
‘were, for the most part, purely technical,
but they sufficed to render the game rather
tame and unexciting despite the important
issues involved.

Laying Down the Law

The match was not one-sided by any
meas, ‘That Clare won by six scores to
tne is due to their superiority in one dep
ment only. Take the forward, centre-
ficld, or half-back lines, and there was
‘scarcely a toss up between the sides in point
‘ot hurting ability. Come to the back divi-
sion, though, and we have the clue to the
relative cuccess and disaster which fell to
Clare and Leix. The Clare defenders were
brilliantly, not to say remarkably, effective.
The Leix backs and custodian’ were pal-
pably nervous, painfully slow, and disas-
trously clumsy in responaing to the aemands
made upoa them by a particularly fast and
tricky forward line. An effective back must
bb: quick to anticipate the line of acuon an
opponent will be likely to cake, In Sune
day's match the Leix backs seemed to be
the last persons in Croke Park to realise
what was occurring until the green flag
waved, denoting the fall of their citadel. L
fclect ho one for blame, but L think itonly

CLUB SUBSCRIPTIONS

A WARNING

In an association like the G.A.A, the
principal source of income to the clubs is
the members’ subscription list. In_many
clubs, however, subscriptions are collected
con a faulty system, and the result leads to
annoyance and dificulty all round. A
‘club's suce.ts upon the Fld is often seriously
rmilitated against by reason of its financial
dificulties, which leave no margin to cover
sudden emergencies. Another source of
discord in regard to subscriprions arises from
the transfer of players rule, whereby a player
to qualify for transfer to another club mnst
have a clean bill as regards bis subscriptious
to his old club. Many clubs issue no sub-
scription cards to their members, and bitter
disputes arise round the transfer of players
by reason of the clubs incomplete record

A LEIX VIEW

Our boys went down before Clare in an
unmistakable manner on Sunday last, al-
though we were expecting great things from
them. We were sadly dissapointed and
those “in the know”

feom previous performances we expected
better results, You might say we never
had a ghost of a chance during the progress
of the game after the first few minutes.
Where the fault lies Tam not going to say,
as the team, on the whole, hurled a poor
and disappointing game, from the usual
game which we are in the habit of seeing

them play. Well there isone thing we must

candidly admit—that we were beaten by a
better team, better in every way, faster,
neater strikers and better tacklers. There
is not one in Leix who will not give Clare
the greatest praise for the game they played
and congratulate them on winning the All-
Ireland hurling champoinship,
